headline: Sarawak Tribal Chiefs Challenge Forest Reclassification
context: Fifteen Penan and Kenyah tribal chiefs filed judicial review in March 2026 against reclassification of 180,000 hectares from permanent forest to state land.

cards.big: 15 tribal chiefs challenge reclassification of 180,000 hectares of forest
cards.sub: Change from permanent forest to state land opens area to logging and plantations

cards.big: Reclassified area in upper Baram contains 25 active indigenous settlements
cards.sub: Approximately 8,000 residents depend on the forest for subsistence

cards.big: 3 timber concessions worth RM1.2 billion issued within 30 days of reclassification
cards.sub: Concessions went to companies linked to individuals in state ruling coalition

cards.big: Sarawak permanent forest reserve shrunk from 6 million to 4.1 million hectares since 1990
cards.sub: Rate of reclassification accelerated 35% between 2020 and 2025

cards.big: Forest reclassification tests whether permanent protection can be quietly reversed
Tribal chiefs challenging forest reclassification defend a land governance system older than the state itself.
